briefing.headline: Rig up a Drop Shot — finesse summer approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Drop Shot is a strong choice for summer with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

Johnston: On the St. Lawrence, fish an 80-yard stretch of bottom in about 40 feet targeting a pod of giant smallmouth. Catch one, then leave the spot and make a big run away before returning, because pressured deepwater smallmouth shut down after each catch.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Shakyhead — finesse summer approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Shakyhead is a strong choice for summer with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Extremely cold (42 degrees or below) and warmer than 90 degrees are prime conditions for a shaky-head. At cold extremes, fish are just up shallow beginning to get aggressive but not ready to eat a jig. A shaky-head is a small weedless finesse bait that's easy for lethargic fish to eat.'

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Squarebill Crankbait — aggressive fall approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for fall with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: 'The dream scenario for a squarebill in fall is a rocky creek channel swing in the back of a major tributary, with shad active near the surface. It's an efficient bait because bass are moving shallow and will bite it in just about any color of water.'

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.gameplan:
The Medium Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for fall with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: The DT-6 is the most universal crankbait; it covers the 3-6 foot sweet spot and works from mid-40s water temps through pre-spawn, post-spawn, summer, and fall on rivers, highland and lowland impoundments, and smallmouth lakes.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

The Flipping Jig is a strong choice for fall with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Texas Rig — aggressive fall approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Texas Rig is a strong choice for fall with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Peg the sinker on baits with a lot of action (craw-style); leave it unpegged on gliding-action baits like tubes so the weight slides away for better glide.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Drop Shot — aggressive fall approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Drop Shot is a strong choice for fall with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

Johnston: On deep Lake Ontario smallmouth at 33 feet, use a drop shot with a 3/8-ounce weight in all conditions. On calm days, cast it out and let it sit — don't move it. On rough, windy days, cast past the fish and drag it through them.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Neko Rig — aggressive fall approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Neko Rig is a strong choice for fall with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: In clear water, use dice/bug baits (Sexy Bug, Tumbleweed) on a Neko rig — incredible action and visibility. In stained water, stick to the traditional Neko worm for a subtler profile. Superglue the tungsten weights so they don't fly out when fish jump.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Shakyhead — aggressive fall approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Shakyhead is a strong choice for fall with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Extremely cold (42 degrees or below) and warmer than 90 degrees are prime conditions for a shaky-head. At cold extremes, fish are just up shallow beginning to get aggressive but not ready to eat a jig. A shaky-head is a small weedless finesse bait that's easy for lethargic fish to eat.'

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Walking Topwater — aggressive fall approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Walking Topwater is a strong choice for fall with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Uses a constant steady retrieve twitching with a little slack; when a fish blows up and misses, continue working the bait - slow it down but snap it harder to make it look injured but still running away from the fish.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Squarebill Crankbait — steady fall approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for fall with clear water and stable conditions.

KVD: 'The dream scenario for a squarebill in fall is a rocky creek channel swing in the back of a major tributary, with shad active near the surface. It's an efficient bait because bass are moving shallow and will bite it in just about any color of water.'

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Medium Diving Crankbait — steady fall approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Medium Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for fall with clear water and stable conditions.

Wheeler: The DT-6 is the most universal crankbait; it covers the 3-6 foot sweet spot and works from mid-40s water temps through pre-spawn, post-spawn, summer, and fall on rivers, highland and lowland impoundments, and smallmouth lakes.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Neko Rig — steady fall approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Neko Rig is a strong choice for fall with clear water and stable conditions.

KVD: In clear water, use dice/bug baits (Sexy Bug, Tumbleweed) on a Neko rig — incredible action and visibility. In stained water, stick to the traditional Neko worm for a subtler profile. Superglue the tungsten weights so they don't fly out when fish jump.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Shakyhead — steady fall approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Shakyhead is a strong choice for fall with clear water and stable conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Extremely cold (42 degrees or below) and warmer than 90 degrees are prime conditions for a shaky-head. At cold extremes, fish are just up shallow beginning to get aggressive but not ready to eat a jig. A shaky-head is a small weedless finesse bait that's easy for lethargic fish to eat.'

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Walking Topwater — steady fall approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Walking Topwater is a strong choice for fall with clear water and stable conditions.

Wheeler: Uses a constant steady retrieve twitching with a little slack; when a fish blows up and misses, continue working the bait - slow it down but snap it harder to make it look injured but still running away from the fish.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Squarebill Crankbait — finesse fall approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for fall with clear water and post frontal conditions.

KVD: 'The dream scenario for a squarebill in fall is a rocky creek channel swing in the back of a major tributary, with shad active near the surface. It's an efficient bait because bass are moving shallow and will bite it in just about any color of water.'

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Drop Shot — finesse fall approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Drop Shot is a strong choice for fall with clear water and post frontal conditions.

Johnston: On deep Lake Ontario smallmouth at 33 feet, use a drop shot with a 3/8-ounce weight in all conditions. On calm days, cast it out and let it sit — don't move it. On rough, windy days, cast past the fish and drag it through them.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Neko Rig — finesse fall approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Neko Rig is a strong choice for fall with clear water and post frontal conditions.

KVD: In clear water, use dice/bug baits (Sexy Bug, Tumbleweed) on a Neko rig — incredible action and visibility. In stained water, stick to the traditional Neko worm for a subtler profile. Superglue the tungsten weights so they don't fly out when fish jump.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Shakyhead — finesse fall approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Shakyhead is a strong choice for fall with clear water and post frontal conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Extremely cold (42 degrees or below) and warmer than 90 degrees are prime conditions for a shaky-head. At cold extremes, fish are just up shallow beginning to get aggressive but not ready to eat a jig. A shaky-head is a small weedless finesse bait that's easy for lethargic fish to eat.'

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Squarebill Crankbait — aggressive fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for fall with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: 'Fall reservoir creek arms — line up with the creek channel edge and fire long casts ahead of the boat. Use the squarebill for surveillance, running down edges, hitting bottom, stumps, laydowns, docks. Work it fast and erratic. Bump everything. 17-20lb fluorocarbon to rip through grass.'

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Spinnerbait — aggressive fall approach for stained water.

The Spinnerbait is a strong choice for fall with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Use a heavier 3/4-ounce spinnerbait in windy conditions — easier to cast and the positive blade resistance makes it easier to feel bites than finesse presentations

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Chatterbait — aggressive fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Chatterbait is a strong choice for fall with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Use a minnow-profile trailer (like CrushCity Freeloader) for natural, erratic movement - it adds realism and bulk without impeding the natural balsa-like hunting action of the ChatterBait.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Flipping Jig — aggressive fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Flipping Jig is a strong choice for fall with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Texas Rig — aggressive fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Texas Rig is a strong choice for fall with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Peg the sinker on baits with a lot of action (craw-style); leave it unpegged on gliding-action baits like tubes so the weight slides away for better glide.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.gameplan:
The Carolina Rig is a strong choice for fall with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Drop Shot — aggressive fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Drop Shot is a strong choice for fall with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

Johnston: On deep Lake Ontario smallmouth at 33 feet, use a drop shot with a 3/8-ounce weight in all conditions. On calm days, cast it out and let it sit — don't move it. On rough, windy days, cast past the fish and drag it through them.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Shakyhead — aggressive fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Shakyhead is a strong choice for fall with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Extremely cold (42 degrees or below) and warmer than 90 degrees are prime conditions for a shaky-head. At cold extremes, fish are just up shallow beginning to get aggressive but not ready to eat a jig. A shaky-head is a small weedless finesse bait that's easy for lethargic fish to eat.'

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.gameplan:
The Walking Topwater is a strong choice for fall with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Uses a constant steady retrieve twitching with a little slack; when a fish blows up and misses, continue working the bait - slow it down but snap it harder to make it look injured but still running away from the fish.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

Palaniuk: 'I like to fish offshore and target shell bars or brushpiles with a 10-inch worm in summer.'
Hackney: 'Big fish relate to the perimeter, a creek channel, and hold on an isolated piece of grass on a flat. Little fish run up shallow while big fish satellite around the area.'

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable fall conditions create the most predictable fall fishing. Bass follow shad migrations into creek arms and pockets, feeding at 3-15ft. Key on points at creek mouths and secondary flats where bait concentrates.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If bites dry up, try shaking in place for 10-15 seconds before moving — patience pays with plastics

briefing.headline: Rig up a Drop Shot — steady fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Drop Shot is a strong choice for fall with stained water and stable conditions.

Johnston: On deep Lake Ontario smallmouth at 33 feet, use a drop shot with a 3/8-ounce weight in all conditions. On calm days, cast it out and let it sit — don't move it. On rough, windy days, cast past the fish and drag it through them.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Ned Rig — steady fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Ned Rig is a strong choice for fall with stained water and stable conditions.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.gameplan:
The Neko Rig is a strong choice for fall with stained water and stable conditions.

Robertson: Pair a Neko rig (size 2 Gamakatsu circle hook, 6.5-inch finesse worm in green-pumpkin) with a Ned rig when targeting smallmouth on deep boulders. Work both presentations in a 60-foot area where fish are concentrated.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Shakyhead — steady fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Shakyhead is a strong choice for fall with stained water and stable conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Extremely cold (42 degrees or below) and warmer than 90 degrees are prime conditions for a shaky-head. At cold extremes, fish are just up shallow beginning to get aggressive but not ready to eat a jig. A shaky-head is a small weedless finesse bait that's easy for lethargic fish to eat.'

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Walking Topwater — steady fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Walking Topwater is a strong choice for fall with stained water and stable conditions.

Wheeler: Uses a constant steady retrieve twitching with a little slack; when a fish blows up and misses, continue working the bait - slow it down but snap it harder to make it look injured but still running away from the fish.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Squarebill Crankbait — finesse fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for fall with stained water and post frontal conditions.

KVD: 'Fall reservoir creek arms — line up with the creek channel edge and fire long casts ahead of the boat. Use the squarebill for surveillance, running down edges, hitting bottom, stumps, laydowns, docks. Work it fast and erratic. Bump everything. 17-20lb fluorocarbon to rip through grass.'

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Swim Jig — finesse fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Swim Jig is a strong choice for fall with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Keep paddle tail swimbaits near the bottom — fishing too far off bottom is the biggest mistake; use lighter line (12-lb fluoro) and heavier head to achieve deeper running depth and better action

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Stop the bait mid-cast and let it fall back toward bottom to increase catch rate by 20-30% — trailing bass can't resist a shad imitator dropping from the school like a dying shad
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal fall fish pull tighter to cover and deeper structure at 8-18ft. The shad migration pauses but doesn't stop. Focus on the deepest fish in a given creek arm — channel bends and main creek points.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If moving baits aren't getting bit, slow down to a crawling retrieve or switch to a jig

briefing.headline: Rig up a Flipping Jig — finesse fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Flipping Jig is a strong choice for fall with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.color: Alabama craw with double header trailer
primaryApproach.retrieve: Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, dock, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Johnston: Don't soak a jig. The more flips you can get in, the more fish you get in front of, the more bites you will get. If they aren't going to eat on the first couple of hops, they can starve.
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, dock, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Football Jig — finesse fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Football Jig is a strong choice for fall with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Separate the two claws on a Punch Out Craw trailer for a football jig, then hop, drag, and deadstick it along the bottom; in stained water the size and radical claw movement is an ideal combination.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Texas Rig — finesse fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Texas Rig is a strong choice for fall with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Peg the sinker on baits with a lot of action (craw-style); leave it unpegged on gliding-action baits like tubes so the weight slides away for better glide.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Carolina Rig — finesse fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Carolina Rig is a strong choice for fall with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Rigs a curly-tail worm (cut paddle tail) on a Carolina rig with 3/4-oz egg sinker, 22-inch fluorocarbon leader (range 18-26 inches), and 3/0 worm hook; retrieves with standard pull-and-drag devoid of deadstick pauses.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Fishes Carolina rig in 4 to 25 feet of water, but most of the time works it in 8 to 12 feet; holds rod to his side at three o'clock position during entire retrieve.
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Drop Shot — finesse fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Drop Shot is a strong choice for fall with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Johnston: On deep Lake Ontario smallmouth at 33 feet, use a drop shot with a 3/8-ounce weight in all conditions. On calm days, cast it out and let it sit — don't move it. On rough, windy days, cast past the fish and drag it through them.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

The Ned Rig is a strong choice for fall with stained water and post frontal conditions.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Neko Rig — finesse fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Neko Rig is a strong choice for fall with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Robertson: Pair a Neko rig (size 2 Gamakatsu circle hook, 6.5-inch finesse worm in green-pumpkin) with a Ned rig when targeting smallmouth on deep boulders. Work both presentations in a 60-foot area where fish are concentrated.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Shakyhead — finesse fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Shakyhead is a strong choice for fall with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Extremely cold (42 degrees or below) and warmer than 90 degrees are prime conditions for a shaky-head. At cold extremes, fish are just up shallow beginning to get aggressive but not ready to eat a jig. A shaky-head is a small weedless finesse bait that's easy for lethargic fish to eat.'

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Squarebill Crankbait — aggressive fall approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for fall with muddy water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: 'The dream scenario for a squarebill in fall is a rocky creek channel swing in the back of a major tributary, with shad active near the surface. It's an efficient bait because bass are moving shallow and will bite it in just about any color of water.'

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal fall bass are following shad into the backs of creeks. Fish can be anywhere from 3-18ft depending on bait position. Target creek arms, secondary points, and anywhere baitfish are concentrating.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure

If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend

briefing.headline: Rig up a Medium Diving Crankbait — aggressive fall approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Medium Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for fall with muddy water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: The DT-6 is the most universal crankbait; it covers the 3-6 foot sweet spot and works from mid-40s water temps through pre-spawn, post-spawn, summer, and fall on rivers, highland and lowland impoundments, and smallmouth lakes.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.gameplan:
The Deep Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for fall with muddy water and pre frontal conditions.

Robertson: When the swimbait bite slows and fish scatter, pick up a deep-diving crankbait like the SPRO Little John DD 70 to put the majority of your weight in the boat. It produced the bulk of a 32-pound bag.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Shakyhead — aggressive fall approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Shakyhead is a strong choice for fall with muddy water and pre frontal conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Extremely cold (42 degrees or below) and warmer than 90 degrees are prime conditions for a shaky-head. At cold extremes, fish are just up shallow beginning to get aggressive but not ready to eat a jig. A shaky-head is a small weedless finesse bait that's easy for lethargic fish to eat.'

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Drop Shot — steady fall approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Drop Shot is a strong choice for fall with muddy water and stable conditions.

Johnston: On deep Lake Ontario smallmouth at 33 feet, use a drop shot with a 3/8-ounce weight in all conditions. On calm days, cast it out and let it sit — don't move it. On rough, windy days, cast past the fish and drag it through them.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Shakyhead — steady fall approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Shakyhead is a strong choice for fall with muddy water and stable conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Extremely cold (42 degrees or below) and warmer than 90 degrees are prime conditions for a shaky-head. At cold extremes, fish are just up shallow beginning to get aggressive but not ready to eat a jig. A shaky-head is a small weedless finesse bait that's easy for lethargic fish to eat.'

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Squarebill Crankbait — finesse fall approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for fall with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

KVD: 'The dream scenario for a squarebill in fall is a rocky creek channel swing in the back of a major tributary, with shad active near the surface. It's an efficient bait because bass are moving shallow and will bite it in just about any color of water.'

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

The Flipping Jig is a strong choice for fall with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

briefing.gameplan:
The Texas Rig is a strong choice for fall with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Peg the sinker on baits with a lot of action (craw-style); leave it unpegged on gliding-action baits like tubes so the weight slides away for better glide.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Drop Shot — finesse fall approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Drop Shot is a strong choice for fall with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

Johnston: On deep Lake Ontario smallmouth at 33 feet, use a drop shot with a 3/8-ounce weight in all conditions. On calm days, cast it out and let it sit — don't move it. On rough, windy days, cast past the fish and drag it through them.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

Johnston: Fall smallmouth fishing is all about location and timing. Cover water fast with reaction baits to find active groups, then slow down with finesse presentations to catch the rest. If fish stop biting a tube, switch to Ned rig or drop shot.

The Neko Rig is a strong choice for fall with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Shakyhead — finesse fall approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Shakyhead is a strong choice for fall with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Extremely cold (42 degrees or below) and warmer than 90 degrees are prime conditions for a shaky-head. At cold extremes, fish are just up shallow beginning to get aggressive but not ready to eat a jig. A shaky-head is a small weedless finesse bait that's easy for lethargic fish to eat.'

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Squarebill Crankbait — aggressive winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: Flatside crankbait (Hardliner 35) is the FIRST crankbait in extreme cold — 'the difference between a flatside and even a squarebill is night and day in the amount of bites in extremely cold water.' Smaller profile, no rattle, but flat sides give off huge pressure waves fish home in on.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal winter bass stage at 12-25ft on main lake structure. Falling pressure before a front is often the best winter fishing window. Fish move slightly shallower and feed more aggressively than normal winter behavior.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend

briefing.gameplan:
The Medium Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: The DT-6 is the most universal crankbait; it covers the 3-6 foot sweet spot and works from mid-40s water temps through pre-spawn, post-spawn, summer, and fall on rivers, highland and lowland impoundments, and smallmouth lakes.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Lipless Crankbait — aggressive winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Lipless Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: In extreme cold on grass lakes, upsize line to 17-20lb fluoro to slow the bait down. Downsize to quarter or 3/8 oz for ultra-shallow flats. Fish it like a jig — short hopping, not just cast and reel. The shimmy/quiver fall is what makes the Red Eye Shad special.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: On Tennessee River-type lakes, fish lipless-crankbait depth (10 feet or less) near grass. On clear highland reservoirs (Cherokee, Percy Priest, Lanier), fish 25-45 feet in main river channels, deepwater drains, and sharp edges near bait c...

KVD: CC3 medium-heavy composite (graphite/glass) cranking rod for lipless crankbaits. 6.2:1 gear ratio reel — not high-speed in cold water. The composite gives feel for detecting grass on hooks while having power to rip free.

Hackney: 'For winter deep-water jig fishing on lowland reservoirs with stumps and channel swings in 6-8 feet, I use 16-20 pound fluorocarbon. On hard structure like rock I go 14-16 pound. Only downsize to 12 in extremely deep clear water'
Robertson: During prespawn at Chickamauga, fish an offshore pattern with a lipless crankbait in grass, then go headhunting with a big swimbait for kicker bites.

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal winter bass stage at 12-25ft on main lake structure. Falling pressure before a front is often the best winter fishing window. Fish move slightly shallower and feed more aggressively than normal winter behavior.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend

briefing.headline: Rig up a Suspending Jerkbait — aggressive winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Suspending Jerkbait is a strong choice for winter with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: KVD 300 Deep runs 10-12ft — go-to for highland reservoirs and clear natural lakes in cold water. Fish off main lake points, bluff walls, bridge pilings, riprap. Parallel deeper edges. Twitch-twitch-pause with LONG pauses. Bright colors in shad kill conditions so fish see it from distance.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, point, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, point, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

Johnston: For smallmouth bass in cold water, they seem to key in on tube jigs or a swimbait better than jerkbaits. For largemouth, suspending jerkbaits and deep-drop jigs are the go-to.

Palaniuk: 'For winter jerkbaits, use a smaller 70-size reel with 7.1:1 gear ratio in cold water, then bump up to 8.1:1 as it warms into spring. The smaller spool means even a faster ratio isn't too fast.'

briefing.headline: Rig up a Spinnerbait — aggressive winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Spinnerbait is a strong choice for winter with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: Slow rolling a spinnerbait around steep rocky banks and laydowns in cold water. First Bassmaster win was in December at Lake Lanier doing exactly this. Use a plastic trailer — 'absolutely paramount' in cold winter months.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Chatterbait — aggressive winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Chatterbait is a strong choice for winter with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: Thunder Cricket in shallow grass even in upper 30s water. Use 3/8 oz (lighter than normal) with straight-tail blade minnow trailer for subtle action. Pull and stop, not steady retrieve. Get it down in old dead vegetation — it holds heat and bass are drawn to it. Tungsten model has smaller blade

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Swim Jig — aggressive winter approach for clear water.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Flipping Jig — aggressive winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Flipping Jig is a strong choice for winter with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Texas Rig — aggressive winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Texas Rig is a strong choice for winter with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Peg the sinker on baits with a lot of action (craw-style); leave it unpegged on gliding-action baits like tubes so the weight slides away for better glide.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.gameplan:
The Carolina Rig is a strong choice for winter with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Drop Shot — aggressive winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Drop Shot is a strong choice for winter with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

Johnston: On deep Lake Ontario smallmouth at 33 feet, use a drop shot with a 3/8-ounce weight in all conditions. On calm days, cast it out and let it sit — don't move it. On rough, windy days, cast past the fish and drag it through them.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Neko Rig — aggressive winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Neko Rig is a strong choice for winter with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: In clear water, use dice/bug baits (Sexy Bug, Tumbleweed) on a Neko rig — incredible action and visibility. In stained water, stick to the traditional Neko worm for a subtler profile. Superglue the tungsten weights so they don't fly out when fish jump.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Shakyhead — aggressive winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Shakyhead is a strong choice for winter with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Extremely cold (42 degrees or below) and warmer than 90 degrees are prime conditions for a shaky-head. At cold extremes, fish are just up shallow beginning to get aggressive but not ready to eat a jig. A shaky-head is a small weedless finesse bait that's easy for lethargic fish to eat.'

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal winter bass stage at 12-25ft on main lake structure. Falling pressure before a front is often the best winter fishing window. Fish move slightly shallower and feed more aggressively than normal winter behavior.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If you get followers without commits, add scent or downsize your hook/weight

briefing.headline: Rig up a Squarebill Crankbait — steady winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with clear water and stable conditions.

KVD: Flatside crankbait (Hardliner 35) is the FIRST crankbait in extreme cold — 'the difference between a flatside and even a squarebill is night and day in the amount of bites in extremely cold water.' Smaller profile, no rattle, but flat sides give off huge pressure waves fish home in on.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable winter conditions position bass at 15-35ft on main lake structure — bluffs, steep points, channel bends. Fish are lethargic and grouped tight. Vertical presentations directly on structure are essential.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend

briefing.headline: Rig up a Medium Diving Crankbait — steady winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Medium Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with clear water and stable conditions.

Wheeler: The DT-6 is the most universal crankbait; it covers the 3-6 foot sweet spot and works from mid-40s water temps through pre-spawn, post-spawn, summer, and fall on rivers, highland and lowland impoundments, and smallmouth lakes.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Deep Diving Crankbait — steady winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Deep Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with clear water and stable conditions.

Robertson: When the swimbait bite slows and fish scatter, pick up a deep-diving crankbait like the SPRO Little John DD 70 to put the majority of your weight in the boat. It produced the bulk of a 32-pound bag.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Lipless Crankbait — steady winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Lipless Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with clear water and stable conditions.

KVD: In extreme cold on grass lakes, upsize line to 17-20lb fluoro to slow the bait down. Downsize to quarter or 3/8 oz for ultra-shallow flats. Fish it like a jig — short hopping, not just cast and reel. The shimmy/quiver fall is what makes the Red Eye Shad special.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Suspending Jerkbait — steady winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Suspending Jerkbait is a strong choice for winter with clear water and stable conditions.

KVD: KVD 300 Deep runs 10-12ft — go-to for highland reservoirs and clear natural lakes in cold water. Fish off main lake points, bluff walls, bridge pilings, riprap. Parallel deeper edges. Twitch-twitch-pause with LONG pauses. Bright colors in shad kill conditions so fish see it from distance.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Spinnerbait — steady winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Spinnerbait is a strong choice for winter with clear water and stable conditions.

KVD: Slow rolling a spinnerbait around steep rocky banks and laydowns in cold water. First Bassmaster win was in December at Lake Lanier doing exactly this. Use a plastic trailer — 'absolutely paramount' in cold winter months.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Chatterbait — steady winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Chatterbait is a strong choice for winter with clear water and stable conditions.

KVD: Thunder Cricket in shallow grass even in upper 30s water. Use 3/8 oz (lighter than normal) with straight-tail blade minnow trailer for subtle action. Pull and stop, not steady retrieve. Get it down in old dead vegetation — it holds heat and bass are drawn to it. Tungsten model has smaller blade

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.gameplan:
The Flipping Jig is a strong choice for winter with clear water and stable conditions.

Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Texas Rig — steady winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Texas Rig is a strong choice for winter with clear water and stable conditions.

briefing.headline: Rig up a 10" Worm — steady winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The 10" Worm is a strong choice for winter with clear water and stable conditions.

For Yamamoto 10-inch grub: use Gamakatsu EWG Superline 5/0 hook with screw-in 1/4oz weight (F rig) and delayed hookset. Straight shank hooks cause missed fish

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Drop Shot — steady winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Drop Shot is a strong choice for winter with clear water and stable conditions.

Johnston: On deep Lake Ontario smallmouth at 33 feet, use a drop shot with a 3/8-ounce weight in all conditions. On calm days, cast it out and let it sit — don't move it. On rough, windy days, cast past the fish and drag it through them.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Ned Rig — steady winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Ned Rig is a strong choice for winter with clear water and stable conditions.

KVD: In clear water, they'll bite the Ned Rig on the fall. Cast out, let it sink on dead slack line — don't tighten up until it hits bottom.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Neko Rig — steady winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Neko Rig is a strong choice for winter with clear water and stable conditions.

KVD: In clear water, use dice/bug baits (Sexy Bug, Tumbleweed) on a Neko rig — incredible action and visibility. In stained water, stick to the traditional Neko worm for a subtler profile. Superglue the tungsten weights so they don't fly out when fish jump.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Shakyhead — steady winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Shakyhead is a strong choice for winter with clear water and stable conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Extremely cold (42 degrees or below) and warmer than 90 degrees are prime conditions for a shaky-head. At cold extremes, fish are just up shallow beginning to get aggressive but not ready to eat a jig. A shaky-head is a small weedless finesse bait that's easy for lethargic fish to eat.'

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Squarebill Crankbait — finesse winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with clear water and post frontal conditions.

KVD: Flatside crankbait (Hardliner 35) is the FIRST crankbait in extreme cold — 'the difference between a flatside and even a squarebill is night and day in the amount of bites in extremely cold water.' Smaller profile, no rattle, but flat sides give off huge pressure waves fish home in on.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Medium Diving Crankbait — finesse winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Medium Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with clear water and post frontal conditions.

Wheeler: The DT-6 is the most universal crankbait; it covers the 3-6 foot sweet spot and works from mid-40s water temps through pre-spawn, post-spawn, summer, and fall on rivers, highland and lowland impoundments, and smallmouth lakes.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Deep Diving Crankbait — finesse winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Deep Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with clear water and post frontal conditions.

Robertson: When the swimbait bite slows and fish scatter, pick up a deep-diving crankbait like the SPRO Little John DD 70 to put the majority of your weight in the boat. It produced the bulk of a 32-pound bag.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Lipless Crankbait — finesse winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Lipless Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with clear water and post frontal conditions.

KVD: In extreme cold on grass lakes, upsize line to 17-20lb fluoro to slow the bait down. Downsize to quarter or 3/8 oz for ultra-shallow flats. Fish it like a jig — short hopping, not just cast and reel. The shimmy/quiver fall is what makes the Red Eye Shad special.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Suspending Jerkbait — finesse winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Suspending Jerkbait is a strong choice for winter with clear water and post frontal conditions.

KVD: KVD 300 Deep runs 10-12ft — go-to for highland reservoirs and clear natural lakes in cold water. Fish off main lake points, bluff walls, bridge pilings, riprap. Parallel deeper edges. Twitch-twitch-pause with LONG pauses. Bright colors in shad kill conditions so fish see it from distance.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Spinnerbait — finesse winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Spinnerbait is a strong choice for winter with clear water and post frontal conditions.

KVD: Slow rolling a spinnerbait around steep rocky banks and laydowns in cold water. First Bassmaster win was in December at Lake Lanier doing exactly this. Use a plastic trailer — 'absolutely paramount' in cold winter months.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Chatterbait — finesse winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Chatterbait is a strong choice for winter with clear water and post frontal conditions.

KVD: Thunder Cricket in shallow grass even in upper 30s water. Use 3/8 oz (lighter than normal) with straight-tail blade minnow trailer for subtle action. Pull and stop, not steady retrieve. Get it down in old dead vegetation — it holds heat and bass are drawn to it. Tungsten model has smaller blade

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Shakyhead — finesse winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Shakyhead is a strong choice for winter with clear water and post frontal conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Extremely cold (42 degrees or below) and warmer than 90 degrees are prime conditions for a shaky-head. At cold extremes, fish are just up shallow beginning to get aggressive but not ready to eat a jig. A shaky-head is a small weedless finesse bait that's easy for lethargic fish to eat.'

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: Flatside crankbait (Hardliner 35) is the FIRST crankbait in extreme cold — 'the difference between a flatside and even a squarebill is night and day in the amount of bites in extremely cold water.' Smaller profile, no rattle, but flat sides give off huge pressure waves fish home in on.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal winter bass stage at 12-25ft on main lake structure. Falling pressure before a front is often the best winter fishing window. Fish move slightly shallower and feed more aggressively than normal winter behavior.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Medium Diving Crankbait — aggressive winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Medium Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: The DT-6 is the most universal crankbait; it covers the 3-6 foot sweet spot and works from mid-40s water temps through pre-spawn, post-spawn, summer, and fall on rivers, highland and lowland impoundments, and smallmouth lakes.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Deep Diving Crankbait — aggressive winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Deep Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

Robertson: When the swimbait bite slows and fish scatter, pick up a deep-diving crankbait like the SPRO Little John DD 70 to put the majority of your weight in the boat. It produced the bulk of a 32-pound bag.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Lipless Crankbait — aggressive winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Lipless Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: In extreme cold on grass lakes, upsize line to 17-20lb fluoro to slow the bait down. Downsize to quarter or 3/8 oz for ultra-shallow flats. Fish it like a jig — short hopping, not just cast and reel. The shimmy/quiver fall is what makes the Red Eye Shad special.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

KVD: CC3 medium-heavy composite (graphite/glass) cranking rod for lipless crankbaits. 6.2:1 gear ratio reel — not high-speed in cold water. The composite gives feel for detecting grass on hooks while having power to rip free.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Suspending Jerkbait — aggressive winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Suspending Jerkbait is a strong choice for winter with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: A jerkbait is the best winter bait. Forward-facing sonar shows that long pauses are important not just because water is colder, but also to give bass time to come up from deeper water to meet the bait. Once the fish is closer, speed up the cadence and they'll usually bite on the next pause.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

Johnston: For smallmouth bass in cold water, they seem to key in on tube jigs or a swimbait better than jerkbaits. For largemouth, suspending jerkbaits and deep-drop jigs are the go-to.

Palaniuk: 'For winter jerkbaits, use a smaller 70-size reel with 7.1:1 gear ratio in cold water, then bump up to 8.1:1 as it warms into spring. The smaller spool means even a faster ratio isn't too fast.'

briefing.headline: Rig up a Chatterbait — aggressive winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Chatterbait is a strong choice for winter with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: Thunder Cricket in shallow grass even in upper 30s water. Use 3/8 oz (lighter than normal) with straight-tail blade minnow trailer for subtle action. Pull and stop, not steady retrieve. Get it down in old dead vegetation — it holds heat and bass are drawn to it. Tungsten model has smaller blade

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Shakyhead — aggressive winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Shakyhead is a strong choice for winter with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Extremely cold (42 degrees or below) and warmer than 90 degrees are prime conditions for a shaky-head. At cold extremes, fish are just up shallow beginning to get aggressive but not ready to eat a jig. A shaky-head is a small weedless finesse bait that's easy for lethargic fish to eat.'

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Squarebill Crankbait — steady winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with stained water and stable conditions.

KVD: Flatside crankbait (Hardliner 35) is the FIRST crankbait in extreme cold — 'the difference between a flatside and even a squarebill is night and day in the amount of bites in extremely cold water.' Smaller profile, no rattle, but flat sides give off huge pressure waves fish home in on.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'I'm a big believer in flat-sided crankbaits in winter — the KVD 1.5 Flat and Lucky Shad for cold water. Throw Lucky Shad on spinning equipment with 8lb fluoro for extra distance and depth.'

Robertson: Spring crankbait progression -- 1) Prespawn: slow-roll lipless on flats near channel banks in 1-6 feet; 2) Postspawn: big-bodied squarebill on tapering points near spawning flats in under 10 feet; 3) Late postspawn: deep diver following...

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable winter conditions position bass at 15-35ft on main lake structure — bluffs, steep points, channel bends. Fish are lethargic and grouped tight. Vertical presentations directly on structure are essential.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend

briefing.headline: Rig up a Medium Diving Crankbait — steady winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Medium Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with stained water and stable conditions.

Wheeler: The DT-6 is the most universal crankbait; it covers the 3-6 foot sweet spot and works from mid-40s water temps through pre-spawn, post-spawn, summer, and fall on rivers, highland and lowland impoundments, and smallmouth lakes.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

The Deep Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with stained water and stable conditions.

Robertson: When the swimbait bite slows and fish scatter, pick up a deep-diving crankbait like the SPRO Little John DD 70 to put the majority of your weight in the boat. It produced the bulk of a 32-pound bag.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Lipless Crankbait — steady winter approach for stained water.

The Lipless Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with stained water and stable conditions.

KVD: In extreme cold on grass lakes, upsize line to 17-20lb fluoro to slow the bait down. Downsize to quarter or 3/8 oz for ultra-shallow flats. Fish it like a jig — short hopping, not just cast and reel. The shimmy/quiver fall is what makes the Red Eye Shad special.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Suspending Jerkbait — steady winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Suspending Jerkbait is a strong choice for winter with stained water and stable conditions.

Wheeler: A jerkbait is the best winter bait. Forward-facing sonar shows that long pauses are important not just because water is colder, but also to give bass time to come up from deeper water to meet the bait. Once the fish is closer, speed up the cadence and they'll usually bite on the next pause.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

alternateApproach.targets: Focus on point, grass, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

Johnston: For smallmouth bass in cold water, they seem to key in on tube jigs or a swimbait better than jerkbaits. For largemouth, suspending jerkbaits and deep-drop jigs are the go-to.

Palaniuk: 'For winter jerkbaits, use a smaller 70-size reel with 7.1:1 gear ratio in cold water, then bump up to 8.1:1 as it warms into spring. The smaller spool means even a faster ratio isn't too fast.'

briefing.headline: Rig up a Chatterbait — steady winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Chatterbait is a strong choice for winter with stained water and stable conditions.

KVD: Thunder Cricket in shallow grass even in upper 30s water. Use 3/8 oz (lighter than normal) with straight-tail blade minnow trailer for subtle action. Pull and stop, not steady retrieve. Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Drop Shot — steady winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Drop Shot is a strong choice for winter with stained water and stable conditions.

Johnston: On deep Lake Ontario smallmouth at 33 feet, use a drop shot with a 3/8-ounce weight in all conditions. On calm days, cast it out and let it sit — don't move it. On rough, windy days, cast past the fish and drag it through them.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Shakyhead — steady winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Shakyhead is a strong choice for winter with stained water and stable conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Extremely cold (42 degrees or below) and warmer than 90 degrees are prime conditions for a shaky-head. At cold extremes, fish are just up shallow beginning to get aggressive but not ready to eat a jig. A shaky-head is a small weedless finesse bait that's easy for lethargic fish to eat.'

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Squarebill Crankbait — finesse winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with stained water and post frontal conditions.

KVD: Flatside crankbait (Hardliner 35) is the FIRST crankbait in extreme cold — 'the difference between a flatside and even a squarebill is night and day in the amount of bites in extremely cold water.' Smaller profile, no rattle, but flat sides give off huge pressure waves fish home in on.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal winter is the toughest bite of the year. Fish retreat to the deepest structure available (20-35ft+) and barely move. Target steep structure where fish can access deep water without traveling — bluffs, vertical channel walls, bridge pilings.

The Medium Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Suspending Jerkbait — finesse winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Suspending Jerkbait is a strong choice for winter with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Wheeler: A jerkbait is the best winter bait. Forward-facing sonar shows that long pauses are important not just because water is colder, but also to give bass time to come up from deeper water to meet the bait. Once the fish is closer, speed up the cadence and they'll usually bite on the next pause.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

The Chatterbait is a strong choice for winter with stained water and post frontal conditions.

KVD: Thunder Cricket in shallow grass even in upper 30s water. Use 3/8 oz (lighter than normal) with straight-tail blade minnow trailer for subtle action. Pull and stop, not steady retrieve. Get it down in old dead vegetation — it holds heat and bass are drawn to it. Tungsten model has smaller blade

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal winter is the toughest bite of the year. Fish retreat to the deepest structure available (20-35ft+) and barely move. Target steep structure where fish can access deep water without traveling — bluffs, vertical channel walls, bridge pilings.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If moving baits aren't getting bit, slow down to a crawling retrieve or switch to a jig

briefing.headline: Rig up a Swim Jig — finesse winter approach for stained water.

The Swim Jig is a strong choice for winter with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Keep paddle tail swimbaits near the bottom — fishing too far off bottom is the biggest mistake; use lighter line (12-lb fluoro) and heavier head to achieve deeper running depth and better action

briefing.headline: Rig up a Flipping Jig — finesse winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Flipping Jig is a strong choice for winter with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Johnston: Don't soak a jig. The more flips you can get in, the more fish you get in front of, the more bites you will get. If they aren't going to eat on the first couple of hops, they can starve.

briefing.gameplan:
The Texas Rig is a strong choice for winter with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Peg the sinker on baits with a lot of action (craw-style); leave it unpegged on gliding-action baits like tubes so the weight slides away for better glide.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Drop Shot — finesse winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Drop Shot is a strong choice for winter with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Johnston: On deep Lake Ontario smallmouth at 33 feet, use a drop shot with a 3/8-ounce weight in all conditions. On calm days, cast it out and let it sit — don't move it. On rough, windy days, cast past the fish and drag it through them.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Shakyhead — finesse winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Shakyhead is a strong choice for winter with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Extremely cold (42 degrees or below) and warmer than 90 degrees are prime conditions for a shaky-head. At cold extremes, fish are just up shallow beginning to get aggressive but not ready to eat a jig. A shaky-head is a small weedless finesse bait that's easy for lethargic fish to eat.'

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Squarebill Crankbait — aggressive winter approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with muddy water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: Flatside crankbait (Hardliner 35) is the FIRST crankbait in extreme cold — 'the difference between a flatside and even a squarebill is night and day in the amount of bites in extremely cold water.' Smaller profile, no rattle, but flat sides give off huge pressure waves fish home in on.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal winter bass stage at 12-25ft on main lake structure. Falling pressure before a front is often the best winter fishing window. Fish move slightly shallower and feed more aggressively than normal winter behavior.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend

briefing.gameplan:
The Medium Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with muddy water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: The DT-6 is the most universal crankbait; it covers the 3-6 foot sweet spot and works from mid-40s water temps through pre-spawn, post-spawn, summer, and fall on rivers, highland and lowland impoundments, and smallmouth lakes.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Deep Diving Crankbait — aggressive winter approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Deep Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with muddy water and pre frontal conditions.

Robertson: When the swimbait bite slows and fish scatter, pick up a deep-diving crankbait like the SPRO Little John DD 70 to put the majority of your weight in the boat. It produced the bulk of a 32-pound bag.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Lipless Crankbait — aggressive winter approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Lipless Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with muddy water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: In extreme cold on grass lakes, upsize line to 17-20lb fluoro to slow the bait down. Downsize to quarter or 3/8 oz for ultra-shallow flats. Fish it like a jig — short hopping, not just cast and reel. The shimmy/quiver fall is what makes the Red Eye Shad special.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

KVD: CC3 medium-heavy composite (graphite/glass) cranking rod for lipless crankbaits. 6.2:1 gear ratio reel — not high-speed in cold water. The composite gives feel for detecting grass on hooks while having power to rip free.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Spinnerbait — aggressive winter approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Spinnerbait is a strong choice for winter with muddy water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: Slow rolling a spinnerbait around steep rocky banks and laydowns in cold water. First Bassmaster win was in December at Lake Lanier doing exactly this. Use a plastic trailer — 'absolutely paramount' in cold winter months.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Chatterbait — aggressive winter approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Chatterbait is a strong choice for winter with muddy water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: Thunder Cricket in shallow grass even in upper 30s water. Use 3/8 oz (lighter than normal) with straight-tail blade minnow trailer for subtle action. Pull and stop, not steady retrieve. Get it down in old dead vegetation — it holds heat and bass are drawn to it. Tungsten model has smaller blade

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Flipping Jig — aggressive winter approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Flipping Jig is a strong choice for winter with muddy water and pre frontal conditions.

Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Johnston: Don't soak a jig. The more flips you can get in, the more fish you get in front of, the more bites you will get. If they aren't going to eat on the first couple of hops, they can starve.

briefing.gameplan:

briefing.headline: Rig up a Drop Shot — aggressive winter approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Drop Shot is a strong choice for winter with muddy water and pre frontal conditions.

Johnston: On deep Lake Ontario smallmouth at 33 feet, use a drop shot with a 3/8-ounce weight in all conditions. On calm days, cast it out and let it sit — don't move it. On rough, windy days, cast past the fish and drag it through them.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Shakyhead — aggressive winter approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Shakyhead is a strong choice for winter with muddy water and pre frontal conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Extremely cold (42 degrees or below) and warmer than 90 degrees are prime conditions for a shaky-head. At cold extremes, fish are just up shallow beginning to get aggressive but not ready to eat a jig. A shaky-head is a small weedless finesse bait that's easy for lethargic fish to eat.'

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

KVD: Flatside crankbait (Hardliner 35) is the FIRST crankbait in extreme cold — 'the difference between a flatside and even a squarebill is night and day in the amount of bites in extremely cold water.' Smaller profile, no rattle, but flat sides give off huge pressure waves fish home in on.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Drop Shot — steady winter approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Drop Shot is a strong choice for winter with muddy water and stable conditions.

Johnston: On deep Lake Ontario smallmouth at 33 feet, use a drop shot with a 3/8-ounce weight in all conditions. On calm days, cast it out and let it sit — don't move it. On rough, windy days, cast past the fish and drag it through them.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Shakyhead — steady winter approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Shakyhead is a strong choice for winter with muddy water and stable conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Extremely cold (42 degrees or below) and warmer than 90 degrees are prime conditions for a shaky-head. At cold extremes, fish are just up shallow beginning to get aggressive but not ready to eat a jig. A shaky-head is a small weedless finesse bait that's easy for lethargic fish to eat.'

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Squarebill Crankbait — finesse winter approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

KVD: Flatside crankbait (Hardliner 35) is the FIRST crankbait in extreme cold — 'the difference between a flatside and even a squarebill is night and day in the amount of bites in extremely cold water.' Smaller profile, no rattle, but flat sides give off huge pressure waves fish home in on.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.gameplan:
The Flipping Jig is a strong choice for winter with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Texas Rig — finesse winter approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Texas Rig is a strong choice for winter with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Peg the sinker on baits with a lot of action (craw-style); leave it unpegged on gliding-action baits like tubes so the weight slides away for better glide.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Drop Shot — finesse winter approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Drop Shot is a strong choice for winter with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

Johnston: On deep Lake Ontario smallmouth at 33 feet, use a drop shot with a 3/8-ounce weight in all conditions. On calm days, cast it out and let it sit — don't move it. On rough, windy days, cast past the fish and drag it through them.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

briefing.headline: Rig up a Shakyhead — finesse winter approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Shakyhead is a strong choice for winter with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Extremely cold (42 degrees or below) and warmer than 90 degrees are prime conditions for a shaky-head. At cold extremes, fish are just up shallow beginning to get aggressive but not ready to eat a jig. A shaky-head is a small weedless finesse bait that's easy for lethargic fish to eat.'

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.
